proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age (higher age_years) is associated with lower probability of objective_response in the overall cohort.
Female sex (sex_female=1) is associated with a different probability of objective_response than male sex.
Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Mean age in responders=67.98 vs non-responders=68.00; diff=-0.02 years; Welch t-test p=0.882.
Response rate: female=0.1891 vs male=0.1862; OR=1.019; chi-square p=0.414.
Logistic regression of objective_response on ecog_ps: log-odds beta=-0.3529 per 1-point increase, p=1.59e-90.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with complex_karyotype have lower objective_response rate than those without.
Patients with tp53_mutation have lower objective_response rate than those without.
Patients with secondary_aml have lower objective_response rate than those with de novo AML.
Patients flagged unfit_for_intensive have lower objective_response rate than fit patients.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate complex_karyotype=1: 0.1681 vs complex_karyotype=0: 0.1909; diff=-0.0229; OR=0.856; chi-square p=3.06e-06.
Response rate tp53_mutation=1: 0.1657 vs tp53_mutation=0: 0.1899; diff=-0.0242; OR=0.847; chi-square p=3.23e-05.
Response rate secondary_aml=1: 0.1881 vs secondary_aml=0: 0.1873; diff=0.0008; OR=1.005; chi-square p=0.841.
Response rate unfit_for_intensive=1: 0.2210 vs unfit_for_intensive=0: 0.1651; diff=0.0559; OR=1.435; chi-square p=1.56e-55.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with flt3_itd mutation have a different (likely lower) overall objective_response rate than wild-type patients.
Patients with flt3_tkd mutation have a different overall objective_response rate than wild-type.
Patients with idh1_mutation have a different overall objective_response rate than wild-type.
Patients with idh2_mutation have a different overall objective_response rate than wild-type.
Patients with npm1_mutation have a higher overall objective_response rate than wild-type (favorable mutation).

analyses.result_summary: Response rate idh1_mutation=1: 0.1909 vs 0: 0.1872; diff=+0.0037; OR=1.025; p=0.581.
Response rate idh2_mutation=1: 0.1915 vs 0: 0.1871; diff=+0.0044; OR=1.029; p=0.462.
Response rate npm1_mutation=1: 0.2346 vs 0: 0.1673; diff=+0.0674; OR=1.526; p=4.64e-70.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher probability of objective_response.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
Higher wbc_k_per_ul is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
Higher blast_pct_marrow is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
Higher nlr (neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with higher probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: albumin_g_dl: mean in responders=3.824 vs non-responders=3.795; logistic beta=0.11745 per 1-unit, p=2.86e-07.
ldh_u_l: mean in responders=223.115 vs non-responders=223.981; logistic beta=-0.00013 per 1-unit, p=0.35.
wbc_k_per_ul: mean in responders=19.272 vs non-responders=20.386; logistic beta=-0.00177 per 1-unit, p=0.000185.
blast_pct_marrow: mean in responders=58.495 vs non-responders=60.296; logistic beta=-0.00492 per 1-unit, p=2.41e-16.
crp_mg_l: mean in responders=5.693 vs non-responders=6.169; logistic beta=-0.00639 per 1-unit, p=6.05e-06.
nlr: mean in responders=3.502 vs non-responders=3.505; logistic beta=-0.00055 per 1-unit, p=0.919.
weight_loss_pct_6mo: mean in responders=3.441 vs non-responders=3.951; logistic beta=-0.03572 per 1-unit, p=1.14e-30.
hemoglobin_g_dl: mean in responders=12.482 vs non-responders=12.512; logistic beta=-0.00919 per 1-unit, p=0.15.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher ast_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher alt_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher total_bilirubin_mg_dl is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher creatinine_mg_dl is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher bun_mg_dl is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher sodium_meq_l (within range) is associated with higher objective_response.
Higher potassium_meq_l is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher calcium_mg_dl is associated with higher objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: alkaline_phosphatase_u_l: logistic beta=0.00045, p=0.0511.
ast_u_l: logistic beta=-0.00150, p=0.128.
alt_u_l: logistic beta=0.00121, p=0.195.
total_bilirubin_mg_dl: logistic beta=-0.04510, p=0.216.
creatinine_mg_dl: logistic beta=-0.01401, p=0.716.
bun_mg_dl: logistic beta=0.00138, p=0.548.
sodium_meq_l: logistic beta=-0.00191, p=0.616.
potassium_meq_l: logistic beta=-0.00685, p=0.764.
calcium_mg_dl: logistic beta=0.00540, p=0.813.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic regression of patient features (no treatment terms), age_years, ecog_ps, complex_karyotype, tp53_mutation, secondary_aml, ldh_u_l, blast_pct_marrow, albumin_g_dl, hemoglobin_g_dl independently predict objective_response with the directions stated above.

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logistic (features only) coefficients — age_years: beta=-0.0003, p=0.767; sex_female: beta=0.0184, p=0.43; ecog_ps: beta=-0.3582, p=9.09e-92; secondary_aml: beta=0.0043, p=0.874; unfit_for_intensive: beta=0.3702, p=7.4e-57; complex_karyotype: beta=-0.1665, p=7.8e-07; flt3_itd: beta=0.0051, p=0.861; flt3_tkd: beta=-0.0503, p=0.327; idh1_mutation: beta=0.0287, p=0.521; idh2_mutation: beta=0.0223, p=0.575; npm1_mutation: beta=0.4328, p=2.72e-71; tp53_mutation: beta=-0.1813, p=7.21e-06; wbc_k_per_ul: beta=-0.0018, p=0.000126; blast_pct_marrow: beta=-0.0051, p=4.74e-17; albumin_g_dl: beta=0.1222, p=1.32e-07; ldh_u_l: beta=-0.0001, p=0.423; crp_mg_l: beta=-0.0065, p=5.11e-06; nlr: beta=0.0011, p=0.846; hemoglobin_g_dl: beta=-0.0082, p=0.204
Adjusted log-odds for age_years: -0.00032, p=0.767.
Adjusted log-odds for ecog_ps: -0.35818, p=9.09e-92.
Adjusted log-odds for complex_karyotype: -0.16652, p=7.8e-07.
Adjusted log-odds for tp53_mutation: -0.18127, p=7.21e-06.
Adjusted log-odds for secondary_aml: 0.00428, p=0.874.
Adjusted log-odds for ldh_u_l: -0.00012, p=0.423.
Adjusted log-odds for blast_pct_marrow: -0.00509, p=4.74e-17.
Adjusted log-odds for albumin_g_dl: 0.12216, p=1.32e-07.
Adjusted log-odds for hemoglobin_g_dl: -0.00820, p=0.204.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_midostaurin have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving treatment_midostaurin.
Patients receiving treatment_gilteritinib have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving treatment_gilteritinib.
Patients receiving treatment_ivosidenib have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving treatment_ivosidenib.
Patients receiving treatment_enasidenib have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving treatment_enasidenib.
Patients receiving treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
Patients receiving treatment_7plus3 have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving treatment_7plus3.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate treatment_midostaurin=1: 0.1857 (n=7545) vs treatment_midostaurin=0: 0.1878 (n=42455); diff=-0.0021; OR=0.986; p=0.661.
Response rate treatment_gilteritinib=1: 0.1813 (n=7545) vs treatment_gilteritinib=0: 0.1886 (n=42455); diff=-0.0073; OR=0.953; p=0.135.
Response rate treatment_ivosidenib=1: 0.1882 (n=3938) vs treatment_ivosidenib=0: 0.1874 (n=46062); diff=+0.0007; OR=1.005; p=0.911.
Response rate treatment_enasidenib=1: 0.1858 (n=3972) vs treatment_enasidenib=0: 0.1876 (n=46028); diff=-0.0018; OR=0.988; p=0.775.
Response rate treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=1: 0.2196 (n=20020) vs treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=0: 0.1661 (n=29980); diff=+0.0535; OR=1.413; p=5.55e-51.
Response rate treatment_7plus3=1: 0.1889 (n=22580) vs treatment_7plus3=0: 0.1863 (n=27420); diff=+0.0026; OR=1.017; p=0.458.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for age, sex, ECOG PS, disease features (secondary AML, fitness, complex karyotype, mutations), and labs, treatment_midostaurin is independently associated with higher objective_response (positive log-odds).

After adjusting for age, sex, ECOG PS, disease features (secondary AML, fitness, complex karyotype, mutations), and labs, treatment_gilteritinib is independently associated with higher objective_response (positive log-odds).

After adjusting for age, sex, ECOG PS, disease features (secondary AML, fitness, complex karyotype, mutations), and labs, treatment_ivosidenib is independently associated with higher objective_response (positive log-odds).

After adjusting for age, sex, ECOG PS, disease features (secondary AML, fitness, complex karyotype, mutations), and labs, treatment_enasidenib is independently associated with higher objective_response (positive log-odds).

After adjusting for age, sex, ECOG PS, disease features (secondary AML, fitness, complex karyotype, mutations), and labs, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is independently associated with higher objective_response (positive log-odds).

After adjusting for age, sex, ECOG PS, disease features (secondary AML, fitness, complex karyotype, mutations), and labs, treatment_7plus3 is independently associated with higher objective_response (positive log-odds).

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted log-odds for treatment_midostaurin = -0.0122, p=0.706.
Adjusted log-odds for treatment_gilteritinib = -0.0474, p=0.147.
Adjusted log-odds for treatment_ivosidenib = 0.0123, p=0.775.
Adjusted log-odds for treatment_enasidenib = -0.0115, p=0.789.
Adjusted log-odds for treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine = 0.3577, p=6.93e-53.
Adjusted log-odds for treatment_7plus3 = 0.0152, p=0.513.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In FLT3-ITD-positive patients (flt3_itd=1), treatment_midostaurin is associated with higher objective_response rate; in FLT3-ITD-negative patients, no benefit is observed.
There is a positive multiplicative interaction between treatment_midostaurin and flt3_itd on objective_response (interaction term beta>0).

analyses.code: df[df['flt3_itd']==1] groupby treatment_midostaurin; df[df['flt3_itd']==0] groupby treatment_midostaurin
analyses.result_summary: FLT3-ITD+: midostaurin response rate 0.1764 vs none 0.1914 (diff -0.0150, p=0.176). FLT3-ITD-: midostaurin response rate 0.1879 vs none 0.1869 (diff +0.0010, p=0.854).
Interaction term (midostaurin × flt3_itd): beta=-0.1063, p=0.195. Main midostaurin beta=0.0066; flt3_itd beta=0.0292.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In FLT3-TKD-positive patients (flt3_tkd=1), treatment_midostaurin is associated with higher objective_response rate; in TKD-negative patients, no benefit is observed.
Positive interaction between treatment_midostaurin and flt3_tkd on objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: FLT3-TKD+: mido response 0.1761 vs none 0.1813 (diff -0.0052, p=0.794). FLT3-TKD-: mido response 0.1863 vs none 0.1882 (diff -0.0019, p=0.702).
Interaction beta=-0.0229, p=0.87.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In FLT3-ITD-positive patients, treatment_gilteritinib is associated with higher objective_response than no gilteritinib; benefit is absent in FLT3-ITD-negative patients.
Positive interaction between treatment_gilteritinib and flt3_itd.
In FLT3-TKD-positive patients, treatment_gilteritinib is associated with higher objective_response than no gilteritinib.

analyses.result_summary: flt3_itd+: gilt response 0.1911 vs none 0.1889 (diff +0.0023, p=0.835). flt3_itd-: gilt response 0.1789 vs none 0.1885 (diff -0.0096, p=0.0763).
flt3_tkd+: gilt response 0.1507 vs none 0.1856 (diff -0.0349, p=0.0866). flt3_tkd-: gilt response 0.1831 vs none 0.1888 (diff -0.0057, p=0.259).
Interaction beta=0.0792, p=0.323.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In IDH1-mutated patients (idh1_mutation=1), treatment_ivosidenib is associated with higher objective_response; in IDH1 wild-type patients, no benefit is observed.
Positive interaction between treatment_ivosidenib and idh1_mutation.

analyses.result_summary: IDH1+: ivosidenib response 0.1544 vs none 0.1942 (diff -0.0399, p=0.0935). IDH1-: ivosidenib response 0.1909 vs none 0.1869 (diff +0.0040, p=0.551).
Interaction beta=-0.3042, p=0.0768.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In IDH2-mutated patients (idh2_mutation=1), treatment_enasidenib is associated with higher objective_response; in IDH2 wild-type patients, no benefit is observed.
Positive interaction between treatment_enasidenib and idh2_mutation.

analyses.result_summary: IDH2+: enasidenib response 0.1949 vs none 0.1912 (diff +0.0037, p=0.865). IDH2-: enasidenib response 0.1849 vs none 0.1873 (diff -0.0024, p=0.726).
Interaction beta=0.0394, p=0.788.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In unfit_for_intensive=1 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with higher objective_response than no ven/aza; effect is smaller or absent in fit patients.
There is a positive interaction between treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine and age_years on objective_response (effect grows with age).
There is a positive interaction between treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine and unfit_for_intensive on objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Unfit+: ven/aza response 0.3081 vs none 0.1630 (diff +0.1451, p=4.89e-130). Fit: ven/aza response 0.1605 vs none 0.1681 (diff -0.0077, p=0.0804).
Interaction beta (per year × treatment)=-0.00144, p=0.497. Main venaza beta=0.4435.
Interaction beta=0.8829, p=1.14e-78.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a negative interaction between treatment_7plus3 and age_years on objective_response (7+3 benefit shrinks or reverses with age).
There is a negative interaction between treatment_7plus3 and ecog_ps on objective_response.
There is a negative interaction between treatment_7plus3 and unfit_for_intensive on objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=0.00122, p=0.564. Main 7+3 beta=-0.0659; age_years beta=-0.0007.
Interaction beta=-0.01529, p=0.663. Main 7+3 beta=0.0304; ecog_ps beta=-0.3461.
Interaction beta=-0.02728, p=0.556. Main 7+3 beta=0.0282; unfit_for_intensive beta=0.3733.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There exist multiple statistically significant treatment × feature interactions on objective_response when all 6 treatments are crossed with patient features (binary mutations, ECOG, age, key labs).

analyses.code: for (t,f) fit Logit(or ~ t + f + t*f); rank by p of interaction
analyses.result_summary: Top 20 strongest treatment×feature interactions by p-value: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine×npm1_mutation: beta=1.055, p=5.5e-102; treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine×unfit_for_intensive: beta=0.883, p=1.1e-78; treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine×tp53_mutation: beta=-0.409, p=4.7e-07; treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine×complex_karyotype: beta=-0.306, p=6e-06; treatment_enasidenib×sex_female: beta=0.314, p=0.00022; treatment_enasidenib×hemoglobin_g_dl: beta=-0.078, p=0.0012; treatment_7plus3×complex_karyotype: beta=-0.213, p=0.0015; treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine×blast_pct_marrow: beta=0.004, p=0.0035; treatment_ivosidenib×npm1_mutation: beta=-0.200, p=0.028; treatment_enasidenib×unfit_for_intensive: beta=0.187, p=0.029; treatment_gilteritinib×unfit_for_intensive: beta=0.135, p=0.037; treatment_ivosidenib×unfit_for_intensive: beta=-0.157, p=0.066; treatment_enasidenib×wbc_k_per_ul: beta=0.003, p=0.074; treatment_ivosidenib×idh1_mutation: beta=-0.304, p=0.077; treatment_enasidenib×secondary_aml: beta=-0.179, p=0.077; treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine×wbc_k_per_ul: beta=0.002, p=0.083; treatment_7plus3×npm1_mutation: beta=-0.075, p=0.12; treatment_ivosidenib×crp_mg_l: beta=0.007, p=0.14; treatment_7plus3×secondary_aml: beta=-0.078, p=0.15; treatment_7plus3×idh1_mutation: beta=0.128, p=0.15
Interaction treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × npm1_mutation: beta=1.0549, p=5.5e-102.
Interaction treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × unfit_for_intensive: beta=0.8829, p=1.14e-78.
Interaction treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × tp53_mutation: beta=-0.4094, p=4.67e-07.
Interaction treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × complex_karyotype: beta=-0.3058, p=6e-06.
Interaction treatment_enasidenib × sex_female: beta=0.3145, p=0.000223.
Interaction treatment_enasidenib × hemoglobin_g_dl: beta=-0.0779, p=0.00116.
Interaction treatment_7plus3 × complex_karyotype: beta=-0.2134, p=0.00151.
Interaction treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × blast_pct_marrow: beta=0.0035, p=0.00351.
Interaction treatment_ivosidenib × npm1_mutation: beta=-0.2000, p=0.0284.
Interaction treatment_enasidenib × unfit_for_intensive: beta=0.1867, p=0.029.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of treatment_gilteritinib is concentrated in flt3_itd=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 patients; in flt3_itd=1 AND tp53_mutation=1 patients the benefit is much smaller or absent.
The benefit of treatment_ivosidenib is concentrated in idh1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 patients.
The benefit of treatment_enasidenib is concentrated in idh2_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 patients.
The benefit of treatment_midostaurin is concentrated in (flt3_itd=1 OR flt3_tkd=1) AND tp53_mutation=0 patients.

analyses.code: stratify by flt3_itd+/tp53- vs flt3_itd+/tp53+; group by treatment_gilteritinib
analyses.result_summary: flt3_itd+/tp53- (n=9020): treatment_gilteritinib response 0.2007 vs none 0.1922 (diff +0.0086, p=0.463). flt3_itd+/tp53+ (n=980): treatment_gilteritinib response 0.1027 vs none 0.1583 (diff -0.0555, p=0.083).

analyses.code: stratify by idh1+/tp53- vs idh1+/tp53+; group by treatment_ivosidenib
analyses.result_summary: idh1+/tp53- (n=3254): treatment_ivosidenib response 0.1476 vs none 0.1934 (diff -0.0458, p=0.0654). idh1+/tp53+ (n=370): treatment_ivosidenib response 0.2222 vs none 0.2012 (diff +0.0211, p=0.793).

analyses.code: stratify by idh2+/tp53- vs idh2+/tp53+; group by treatment_enasidenib
analyses.result_summary: idh2+/tp53- (n=4189): treatment_enasidenib response 0.2038 vs none 0.1920 (diff +0.0118, p=0.609). idh2+/tp53+ (n=490): treatment_enasidenib response 0.1143 vs none 0.1846 (diff -0.0703, p=0.296).

analyses.code: stratify by FLT3+/tp53- vs FLT3+/tp53+; group by treatment_midostaurin
analyses.result_summary: FLT3+/tp53- (n=11564): treatment_midostaurin response 0.1751 vs none 0.1944 (diff -0.0193, p=0.0592). FLT3+/tp53+ (n=1251): treatment_midostaurin response 0.1886 vs none 0.1413 (diff +0.0473, p=0.102).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of treatment_gilteritinib in flt3_itd=1 patients is reduced or abolished when complex_karyotype=1.
The benefit of treatment_ivosidenib in idh1_mutation=1 patients is reduced or abolished when complex_karyotype=1.
The benefit of treatment_enasidenib in idh2_mutation=1 patients is reduced or abolished when complex_karyotype=1.

analyses.code: stratify by flt3_itd+/CK- and flt3_itd+/CK+; group by treatment_gilteritinib
analyses.result_summary: flt3_itd+/CK- (n=8539): treatment_gilteritinib response 0.1925 vs none 0.1897 (diff +0.0028, p=0.814). flt3_itd+/CK+ (n=1461): treatment_gilteritinib response 0.1835 vs none 0.1842 (diff -0.0007, p=0.979).

analyses.code: stratify by idh1_mutation+/CK- and idh1_mutation+/CK+; group by treatment_ivosidenib
analyses.result_summary: idh1_mutation+/CK- (n=3057): treatment_ivosidenib response 0.1660 vs none 0.1942 (diff -0.0283, p=0.285). idh1_mutation+/CK+ (n=567): treatment_ivosidenib response 0.1053 vs none 0.1941 (diff -0.0889, p=0.101).

analyses.code: stratify by idh2_mutation+/CK- and idh2_mutation+/CK+; group by treatment_enasidenib
analyses.result_summary: idh2_mutation+/CK- (n=4003): treatment_enasidenib response 0.2117 vs none 0.1951 (diff +0.0167, p=0.48). idh2_mutation+/CK+ (n=676): treatment_enasidenib response 0.0851 vs none 0.1685 (diff -0.0834, p=0.135).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine in unfit_for_intensive=1 patients is reduced when tp53_mutation=1 (i.e., concentrated in unfit AND tp53 wild-type).
The benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine in unfit_for_intensive=1 patients is reduced when complex_karyotype=1.
In patients age >= 65, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with higher objective_response than no ven/aza; the difference is smaller or absent in age < 65.

analyses.result_summary: unfit/tp53- (n=17980): venaza response 0.3242 vs none 0.1619 (diff +0.1623, p=8.75e-143). unfit/tp53+ (n=2076): venaza response 0.1766 vs none 0.1728 (diff +0.0038, p=0.82).
unfit/CK- (n=17077): venaza response 0.3300 vs none 0.1632 (diff +0.1668, p=5.89e-142). unfit/CK+ (n=2979): venaza response 0.1849 vs none 0.1619 (diff +0.0230, p=0.101).
age>=65: venaza response 0.2187 vs none 0.1662 (diff +0.0525, p=1.05e-30). age<65: venaza response 0.2210 vs none 0.1659 (diff +0.0551, p=5.25e-22).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The objective-response benefit of treatment_7plus3 (vs no 7+3) is concentrated in patients who are fit (unfit_for_intensive=0) AND age<65 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0; the benefit is smaller or absent outside this subgroup.
In unfit_for_intensive=0 patients, treatment_7plus3 is associated with higher objective_response than no 7+3.

analyses.code: subgroup: fit & age<65 & tp53-=0 & CK=0; vs complement
analyses.result_summary: Clean subgroup (n=8938): 7+3 response 0.1683 vs none 0.1604 (diff +0.0079, p=0.314). Complement (n=41062): 7+3 response 0.1934 vs none 0.1920 (diff +0.0013, p=0.732).
Fit subgroup (n=29944): 7+3 response 0.1672 vs none 0.1633 (diff +0.0039, p=0.368).

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_gilteritinib is associated with higher objective_response specifically in patients who are flt3_itd=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0; outside this triple subgroup, gilteritinib provides little to no benefit.
treatment_ivosidenib is associated with higher objective_response specifically in patients who are idh1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0.
treatment_enasidenib is associated with higher objective_response specifically in patients who are idh2_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0.
treatment_midostaurin is associated with higher objective_response specifically in patients who are (flt3_itd=1 OR flt3_tkd=1) AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0.

analyses.result_summary: flt3_itd+ & tp53-/CK- (n=7682): treatment_gilteritinib response 0.2031 vs none 0.1931 (diff +0.0100, p=0.429). Complement (n=42318): treatment_gilteritinib response 0.1774 vs none 0.1878 (diff -0.0104, p=0.0494).

idh1+ & tp53-/CK- (n=2754): treatment_ivosidenib response 0.1598 vs none 0.1937 (diff -0.0339, p=0.221). Complement (n=47246): treatment_ivosidenib response 0.1898 vs none 0.1871 (diff +0.0028, p=0.679).

idh2+ & tp53-/CK- (n=3577): treatment_enasidenib response 0.2202 vs none 0.1964 (diff +0.0239, p=0.339). Complement (n=46423): treatment_enasidenib response 0.1832 vs none 0.1870 (diff -0.0038, p=0.574).

FLT3+ & tp53-/CK- (n=9832): treatment_midostaurin response 0.1791 vs none 0.1967 (diff -0.0177, p=0.112). Complement (n=40168): treatment_midostaurin response 0.1873 vs none 0.1856 (diff +0.0017, p=0.758).

proposed_hypotheses.text: An exhaustive scan of pairs of binary patient features within each treatment identifies subgroups (defined by 2 binary features set to specific values) where the treatment effect on objective_response is much larger than in the complement.

analyses.code: for each treatment, for each (binary f1, binary f2, v1∈{0,1}, v2∈{0,1}) compute response diff(t=1 vs t=0)

proposed_hypotheses.text: In secondary_aml=1 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with higher objective_response than no ven/aza; effect may differ in de novo AML.
In secondary_aml=1 patients, treatment_7plus3 is associated with lower objective_response benefit than in de novo (secondary_aml=0) patients.

analyses.code: stratify by secondary_aml; group by venaza
analyses.result_summary: secondary_aml=1 (n=12301): venaza response 0.2211 vs none 0.1659 (diff +0.0552, p=1.6e-14). secondary_aml=0 (n=37699): venaza response 0.2191 vs none 0.1661 (diff +0.0530, p=4.01e-38).
secondary_aml=1 (n=12301): 7+3 response 0.1847 vs none 0.1910 (diff -0.0063, p=0.371). secondary_aml=0 (n=37699): 7+3 response 0.1903 vs none 0.1848 (diff +0.0055, p=0.172).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a single multivariable logistic model that simultaneously includes all targetable mutations, all 6 treatments, and the four canonical pharmacology interactions (midostaurin×flt3_itd, midostaurin×flt3_tkd, gilteritinib×flt3_itd, ivosidenib×idh1_mutation, enasidenib×idh2_mutation), each canonical interaction has positive coefficient with p<0.05 after adjusting for age, ECOG, fitness, secondary_aml, complex_karyotype, tp53_mutation, npm1_mutation, and key labs.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction term mido_x_itd: beta=-0.1117, p=0.182.
Adjusted interaction term mido_x_tkd: beta=-0.0945, p=0.507.
Adjusted interaction term gilt_x_itd: beta=0.0652, p=0.423.
Adjusted interaction term ivo_x_idh1: beta=-0.3229, p=0.0637.
Adjusted interaction term ena_x_idh2: beta=0.0254, p=0.864.
Adjusted main effect treatment_midostaurin: beta=0.0200, p=0.594 (interpretable as effect when relevant marker=0, holding others fixed).
Adjusted main effect treatment_gilteritinib: beta=-0.0613, p=0.0958 (interpretable as effect when relevant marker=0, holding others fixed).
Adjusted main effect treatment_ivosidenib: beta=0.0391, p=0.381 (interpretable as effect when relevant marker=0, holding others fixed).
Adjusted main effect treatment_enasidenib: beta=-0.0155, p=0.732 (interpretable as effect when relevant marker=0, holding others fixed).
Adjusted main effect treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine: beta=0.3577, p=7.41e-53 (interpretable as effect when relevant marker=0, holding others fixed).
Adjusted main effect treatment_7plus3: beta=0.0136, p=0.56 (interpretable as effect when relevant marker=0, holding others fixed).

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL (ven/aza): treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response specifically in patients with npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0; outside this fully-specified subgroup the marginal effect is small or null. The unfavorable values tp53_mutation=1 and complex_karyotype=1 each independently suppress the effect.

Refined ven/aza hypothesis isolating npm1: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response in npm1_mutation=1 patients (independent of fitness); the largest absolute effect is observed in npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1.
Refined ven/aza hypothesis isolating fitness: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response in unfit_for_intensive=1 patients with tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0.

FINAL (gilteritinib): contrary to prior pharmacology-based expectation, treatment_gilteritinib does NOT meaningfully increase objective_response in any pre-specified subgroup tested (flt3_itd=1, flt3_itd=1 AND tp53=0 AND CK=0, flt3_tkd=1); marginal subgroup effects are near zero and not statistically significant.

FINAL (ivosidenib): contrary to expectation, treatment_ivosidenib does NOT increase objective_response in idh1_mutation=1 patients (subgroup diff is mildly negative); no subgroup with positive significant effect was identified.

FINAL (enasidenib): contrary to expectation, treatment_enasidenib has only a small, non-significant trend toward higher response in idh2_mutation=1 AND tp53=0 AND CK=0; the cleanest subgroup signal is a sex_female × enasidenib interaction (treatment_enasidenib appears more effective in female patients).

FINAL (midostaurin): contrary to expectation, treatment_midostaurin does NOT increase objective_response in FLT3-positive (flt3_itd=1 OR flt3_tkd=1) AND tp53=0 AND CK=0 patients in this cohort; subgroup diffs are slightly negative.

FINAL (7+3): treatment_7plus3 does NOT show a clinically or statistically meaningful increase in objective_response in any subgroup tested, including fit/age<65/tp53=0/CK=0; consistent with the cohort being dominated by unfit/elderly patients in whom 7+3 is ineffective. The only significant heterogeneity is a negative interaction with complex_karyotype (worse with 7+3 in CK+).
